2603.18123.title: Understanding Task Aggregation for Generalizable Ultrasound Foundation Models
2603.18123.paper_summary: Foundation models promise to unify multiple clinical tasks within a single framework, but recent ultrasound studies report that unified models can underperform task-specific baselines. We hypothesize that this degradation arises not from model capacity limitations, but from task aggregation strategies that ignore interactions between task heterogeneity and available training data scale. In this work, we systematically analyze when heterogeneous ultrasound tasks can be jointly learned without performance loss, establishing practical criteria for task aggregation in unified clinical imaging models. We introduce M2DINO, a multi-organ, multi-task framework built on DINOv3 with task-conditioned Mixture-of-Experts blocks for adaptive capacity allocation. We systematically evaluate 27 ultrasound tasks spanning segmentation, classification, detection, and regression under three paradigms: task-specific, clinically-grouped, and all-task unified training. Our results show that aggregation effectiveness depends strongly on training data scale. While clinically-grouped training can improve performance in data-rich settings, it may induce substantial negative transfer in low-data settings. In contrast, all-task unified training exhibits more consistent performance across clinical groups. We further observe that task sensitivity varies by task type in our experiments: segmentation shows the largest performance drops compared with regression and classification. These findings provide practical guidance for ultrasound foundation models, emphasizing that aggregation strategies should jointly consider training data availability and task characteristics rather than relying on clinical taxonomy alone.
2603.18123.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型有望在單一框架內統一多個臨床任務，但最近的超聲研究報告指出，統一模型的表現可能不如特定任務的基準。我們假設這種性能下降並非源於模型能力的限制，而是來自忽視任務異質性與可用訓練數據規模之間互動的任務聚合策略。在本研究中，我們系統性地分析了何時異質的超聲任務可以在不損失性能的情況下共同學習，並建立了統一臨床影像模型中任務聚合的實用標準。我們引入了M2DINO，一個基於DINOv3的多器官、多任務框架，具有基於任務的混合專家模塊以進行自適應容量分配。我們系統性地評估了27個超聲任務，涵蓋分割、分類、檢測和回歸，並在三種範式下進行：特定任務、臨床分組和全任務統一訓練。我們的結果顯示，聚合的有效性強烈依賴於訓練數據的規模。雖然臨床分組訓練可以在數據豐富的環境中提高性能，但在低數據環境中可能會引起顯著的負轉移。相對而言，全任務統一訓練在不同臨床組之間表現出更一致的性能。我們進一步觀察到，在我們的實驗中，任務敏感性因任務類型而異：與回歸和分類相比，分割顯示出最大的性能下降。這些發現為超聲基礎模型提供了實用指導，強調聚合策略應共同考慮訓練數據的可用性和任務特徵，而不僅僅依賴於臨床分類法。
2603.18123.author: Fangyijie Wang et.al.
2603.18123.authors: Fangyijie Wang, Tanya Akumu, Vien Ngoc Dang, Amelia Jimńez-Sánchez, Jieyun Bai, Guénolé Silvestre, Karim Lekadir, Kathleen M. Curran
